exit_criteria: Accepted GC feedback rows are represented in system KB.
KB docs distinguish archive commit from later GC commit.
Executable code/skill work is routed to planning rather than implemented during documentation.
Required stale-reference audit blockers are cleared or explicitly routed.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Documentation validation can use deterministic audits and direct KB/build refs; no code execution boundary changed.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Tracked `.codewiki` artifacts must not be purged until an archive commit containing revive context exists.
GC must write restore evidence that names archive commit/tree, removed paths, and restore commands before deleting tracked artifacts from the hot tree.
Agent skills must make post-commit GC review mandatory at close/publication boundaries and require block/defer evidence when skipped.
A public source-owned GC path must support dry-run, scoped purge, tracked artifact gates, and ignored runtime cleanup without manual file deletion.

evidence_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/builds.md and .codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md define archive commit before tracked purge and separate GC commit.
.codewiki/kb/system/validation-gateway.md states pass reports can be purged only after reachable archive proof and restore evidence, while current/fail/block/policy-kept reports remain hot.
.codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md requires agents to run/defer GC review; .codewiki/kb/system/api.md adds codewiki.gc capability and forbids manual tracked deletion.
.codewiki/kb/system/graph.md makes GC classification advisory until archive proof and calls for post-commit GC next-action candidates.
.codewiki/kb/system/file-structure.md and skills/codewiki/bootstrap/onboarding.md avoid wording that stale-reference audit interpreted as generated task views being truth.

summary: Document post-commit CodeWiki garbage collection protocol and API ownership
knowledge_changes: Updated builds spec with post-commit GC lifecycle and restore ledger requirements.
Updated roadmap spec with agent-owned post-close/post-publication GC review obligation.
Updated validation gateway spec with pass-report purge safety boundaries and close/publication proof relationship.
Updated graph spec with archive-proof gating for purgeability and GC next-action expectations.
Updated API spec with codewiki.gc capability and safe tracked deletion rules.
Updated file-structure wording to keep generated task pages separate from canonical inputs while satisfying stale-reference audit.
roadmap_changes: Executable work remains: implement source-owned GC tool/path, skill enforcement, and tests.
assumptions: Archive commits remain reachable long enough for restore commands to work.
Ignored runtime/session artifacts can have weaker restore guarantees than tracked canonical/build/validation artifacts.
